items.title: CMS issues interim final rule: Medicaid work requirement set at 80 hours/month, states must implement by Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS issued an interim final rule on June 1, 2026 implementing an 80 hours-per-month work requirement ("community engagement") for certain Medicaid adults. The rule says states must generally implement by Jan. 1, 2027 and requires a 30-calendar-day cure period if the state cannot verify compliance or an exemption; it applies to non-pregnant adults ages 19–64 not entitled to or enrolled in Medicare, with exemptions including pregnancy/postpartum and medical frailty.

items.title: USDA finalizes SNAP retailer stocking standards: 7 varieties per staple category; compliance required by Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA published a final rule updating SNAP retailer stocking standards, requiring most SNAP-authorized retailers to carry at least 7 varieties in each of 4 staple food categories (dairy, fruits/vegetables, grains, and protein). The rule also requires at least 1 perishable variety in at least 3 of the 4 categories and sets Nov. 4, 2026 as the compliance date for SNAP-authorized retailers.

items.title: USDA seeks comments on SNAP quality-control form revisions (FNS-245); comments due July 31, 2026
items.summary: USDA published a notice requesting public comment on a proposed information collection for the SNAP FNS-245 Negative Case Action Review Schedule, with comments due July 31, 2026. The notice says the estimated reporting and recordkeeping burden would increase from about 112,491.43 hours to 121,597 hours.

items.title: HUD announces $158 million Section 811 Project Rental Assistance NOFO; applications due July 13, 2026
items.summary: HUD announced a Notice of Funding Opportunity for the Section 811 Project Rental Assistance (PRA) program totaling $158 million. The NOFO aims to expand project-based rental assistance and voluntary supportive services for extremely low-income adults with disabilities, with applications due July 13, 2026.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: SSA posts 2026–2027 payment schedule: SSI on the 1st; Social Security paid on the 2nd/3rd/4th Wednesday by birthday
items.summary: SSA published its 2026–2027 benefit payment calendar: SSI is generally paid on the 1st of the month, while Social Security retirement/disability benefits are typically paid on the second, third, or fourth Wednesday depending on the beneficiary’s birthday (1st–10th, 11th–20th, 21st–31st). The publication also notes that people who started receiving Social Security before May 1997 generally receive payments on the 3rd.

items.title: Education Department guidance highlights Workforce Pell eligibility starting July 1, 2026 (short-term programs 150–599 clock hours)
items.summary: A Federal Student Aid Partner Connect briefing on Pell updates says Workforce Pell eligibility begins July 1, 2026 for eligible workforce programs that are between 150 and 599 clock hours and at least 8 weeks but less than 15 weeks. The document also gives an example maximum Pell amount of $7,395 and notes a new rule making students ineligible if their Student Aid Index (SAI) equals or exceeds twice the maximum (example threshold 14,790).

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2026–27 FAFSA is available for students attending school July 1, 2026–June 30, 2027
items.summary: Federal Student Aid announced that the 2026–27 FAFSA form is available for students seeking aid for attendance dates from July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. Applicants should file as early as possible because states and schools often use priority deadlines even though the form applies to the full aid year.
